summary: Dark Star marathon & Weather Report Suite anchor stellar second set
blurb: The second set delivers a Dark Star/Mind Left Body jam that rolls into Eyes of the World and Stella Blue, highlighted by Jerry’s soaring solos. Weather Report Suite, Let It Grow and One More Saturday Night add depth, while the first set supplies solid work on Playing In The Band and Bertha.
review: Madison’s Dane County Coliseum on October 25, 1973 captured one of the year’s most compelling shows. After a modest first set that builds momentum with tight renditions of Bertha, Here Comes Sunshine and a driving Playing In The Band, the band erupts in the second set. A sprawling Dark Star segues into an inspired Mind Left Body jam, morphs through a Dark Star Jam into an ecstatic Eyes Of The World before melting into a heartfelt Stella Blue – all underscored by Jerry’s “gorgeous, soulful” solos that many fans call among the tour’s best. The Weather Report Suite and Let It Grow showcase Keith’s magical keyboards and Phil’s “crazy bass sound,” while Bill’s snappy drumming drives the band forward. Bob’s vocal flourishes on Saturday Night close the night with unmistakable joy, and the encore Uncle John’s Band leaves the audience humming. Across multiple soundboard sources the performance feels locked‑in, making this a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead in peak ’73 form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 out of 8) are marked positive, praising the Dark Star marathon, Eyes of the World, and Weather Report Suite. No negative sentiment appears; mixed reviews only note minor recording quirks. Consistent high praise across multiple sources justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good overall soundboard capture that preserves the second‑set jam fest while remaining the most complete and accessible version for listeners.

key_highlights: Dark Star marathon with Mind Left Body Jam and Eyes Of The World
Weather Report Suite and Let It Grow showcase deep improvisation
Stella Blue’s emotive finish to the second set
Jerry’s soaring solos and Keith’s magical keyboards

band_performance.Jerry: His soaring, soulful solos on Dark Star, Eyes of the World and Stella Blue are repeatedly called gorgeous and out‑of‑this‑world; a wild string‑bending climax during the Weather Report Suite earns special praise.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a ‘crazy bass sound’ with standout licks in Eyes of the World; his foundation is solid throughout, though occasional clipping is noted.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers energetic vocal support on Bertha and strong, enthusiastic flourishes on One More Saturday Night, adding extra lift to the set’s climax.
band_performance.Keith: Magical keyboard work shines especially during Weather Report Suite, with some reviewers noting early‑set moments feel a bit disjointed but overall very supportive.
band_performance.Bill: Snappy, driving drumming underpins the extended jams, giving the band a steady rhythmic backbone.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
